title: Raw-address pool filters are never normalized on direct loads

evidence.quote: const swapVars = poolFilter ? { poolId: poolFilter, limit } : { limit };

reasoning: The input placeholder and resolver support raw 0x pool addresses, but normalization only runs when the user clicks Apply. A direct visit to /pools?pool=0x... or an Apply before all network pools have loaded leaves poolFilter as the raw address, and the page sends POOL_SWAPS with that raw value. The indexer pool IDs used by this route are namespaced chainId-address IDs, so the query returns no swaps even after the pool list later contains the matching namespaced ID.
reproduction: Open /pools?pool=0xBC69212B8E4D445B2307C9D32Dd68E2A4Df00115 for a pool whose stored id is 143-0xBC69212B8E4D445B2307C9D32Dd68E2A4Df00115. The page issues PoolSwaps with poolId set to the raw address and renders the empty state instead of the pool's swaps.
recommendation: Derive a normalized activePoolFilter from poolFilter plus poolsByNamespacedId and use that for the swaps heading/query. If a raw address cannot yet be resolved because pools are still loading, defer the pool-specific swaps query until resolution completes or re-run normalization once entries arrive.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The included test only covers an already-namespaced Monad filter. It never seeds mockSearchParams with a raw 0x address, so it cannot catch that direct URL state bypasses resolvePoolFilter.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a /pools test with mockSearchParams = new URLSearchParams("pool=0xBC69212B8E4D445B2307C9D32Dd68E2A4Df00115") and mocked networkData containing the 143-namespaced pool; assert PoolSwaps receives { poolId: "143-0xBC69212B8E4D445B2307C9D32Dd68E2A4Df00115", limit: 25 }.

history.note: Fixed by deriving an active pool filter from the URL raw address plus loaded pool metadata before querying swaps; added direct-load raw address regression.
